steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two inventors. The notes show both Beard and Jones improved on existing designs — that shared characteristic is what the correct choice must highlight.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Beard's automatic coupler and Jones's mobile refrigeration system both improved on existing designs.: Directly names both inventors and states the characteristic they share. ✓
  • Choice B — In 1897, Beard invented an automatic coupler, which made the job of connecting train cars safer.: Covers only Beard; Jones is absent, so no similarity is established. ✗
  • Choice C — Beard's invention made the job of connecting train cars safer, whereas Jones's invention enabled food transport trucks to carry perishables farther.: Uses “whereas” to contrast the two inventors, not to emphasize similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — Jones's mobile refrigeration system, which he invented in 1938, made it possible for food transport trucks to carry perishable foods farther.: Covers only Jones; Beard is absent, so no similarity is established. ✗
That is the correct answer.
